subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Supported range** — Base game supports **2-6 players**.
- **2-player setup change** — Set aside **1 facedown + 3 faceup** cards before dealing.
- **3/4/5/6-player setup** — Set aside **1 facedown** card before dealing.
- **Win thresholds by count** — 2P=6 favor, 3P=5 favor, 4P=4 favor, 5P=3 favor, 6P=3 favor.
- **Classic version range** — Classic variant supports **2-4 players** only.
- **Classic card removals** — Remove 1 Guard, 2 Chancellors, 2 Spies.

#### Character Cards (21 total)
- **Spy (0)** — 2 cards.
- **Guard (1)** — 6 cards.
- **Priest (2)** — 2 cards.
- **Baron (3)** — 2 cards.
- **Handmaid (4)** — 2 cards.
- **Prince (5)** — 2 cards.
- **Chancellor (6)** — 2 cards.
- **King (7)** — 1 card.
- **Countess (8)** — 1 card.
- **Princess (9)** — 1 card.

#### Other Components
- **Reference cards** — 6 cards.
- **Favor tokens** — 13 tokens.
- **Cloth bag** — 1 bag.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Setup
- **Reference card** — Give each player 1 reference card.
- **Deck prep** — Shuffle all 21 character cards into one facedown deck.
- **Starter hand** — Deal each player exactly 1 card.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2 Players ---
- **Set-aside cards** — Set aside top 1 card facedown and 3 additional cards faceup.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Set-aside cards** — Set aside top 1 card facedown.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Set-aside cards** — Set aside top 1 card facedown.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Set-aside cards** — Set aside top 1 card facedown.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Set-aside cards** — Set aside top 1 card facedown.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Round Start
- **First player rule** — Player who most recently hand-wrote a letter goes first.
- **Turn direction** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Next Round Start
- **Round winner starts** — Winner of previous round takes first turn.
- **Tied round start** — If previous round tied, randomly choose first player among tied players.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Draw** — Draw 1 card from the deck.
2. **Choose** — Pick 1 of your 2 cards to play faceup.
3. **Resolve** — Resolve the played card's effect completely.
4. **Keep** — Keep the other card in your hand.
5. **Pass** — Pass turn clockwise.

#### If You Are Out of the Round
- **Skip turn** — Your turn is skipped until next round.
- **Target immunity** — You cannot be chosen by card effects until next round.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Core Play Rule
- **One card per turn** — You must play exactly 1 card each turn after drawing.

#### Card Effects
- **Princess (9)** — If played or discarded for any reason, you are immediately out.
- **Countess (8)** — Has no effect when played/discarded.
- **Countess forced-play rule** — You must play Countess if your other card is King or Prince.
- **Countess exception** — Forced-play rule does not apply during temporary Chancellor extra draws.
- **King (7)** — Choose another player and trade hands.
- **Chancellor (6)** — Draw 2 cards, keep 1 of 3 in hand, put 2 on bottom in any order.
- **Chancellor low-deck rule** — If only 1 card remains, draw 1 and return 1 to bottom.
- **Chancellor empty-deck rule** — If no cards remain, card resolves with no effect.
- **Prince (5)** — Choose any player (including yourself); chosen player discards hand and draws new hand.
- **Prince empty-deck rule** — If deck is empty, chosen player draws the facedown set-aside card.
- **Handmaid (4)** — Until start of your next turn, other players cannot choose you for effects.
- **Baron (3)** — Choose another player; secretly compare hands; lower value is out; tie means no one is out.
- **Priest (2)** — Choose another player; secretly look at their hand.
- **Guard (1)** — Choose another player; name a non-Guard character; if correct, that player is out.
- **Spy (0)** — No immediate effect when played/discarded.

#### Handmaid Corner Cases
- **Another-player required cards** — Guard/Priest/Baron/King have no effect if all valid targets are Handmaid-protected.
- **Any-player required card** — Prince must target yourself if all other in-round players are protected.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Out of the Round Procedure
- **Discard timing** — Discard your hand faceup immediately when eliminated.
- **No effect on discard** — Do not resolve the discarded card's effect when eliminated.
- **Reminder aid** — Flip reference card to broken-seal side as elimination reminder.

#### Open Information Rule
- **Visibility** — All played and discarded cards stay visible to all players.

#### Spy Bonus Rule
- **Trigger** — At round end, if you are the only in-round player who played/discarded a Spy, gain 1 favor token.
- **Extra token nature** — Spy token does not replace the normal round-win token.
- **Cap** — You gain at most 1 Spy token even if you played/discarded 2 Spies.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-4 Players (Classic Version Option) ---
- **Classic removals** — Remove 1 Guard, 2 Chancellors, and 2 Spies before setup.
- **Rule continuity** — All other rules remain unchanged.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Round End Triggers
- **Deck exhausted** — If deck is empty after a turn, remaining players reveal hands; highest value wins round.
- **Single survivor** — If only one player remains in round, that player wins round immediately.

#### Round Rewards
- **Round-win token** — Round winner gains 1 favor token.
- **Tie reward** — If highest hand is tied, each tied player gains 1 favor token.
- **Spy bonus token** — Apply Spy bonus token if Spy condition is met.

#### Game End Thresholds
--- 2 Players ---
- **Win threshold** — First to 6 favor tokens wins.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Win threshold** — First to 5 favor tokens wins.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Win threshold** — First to 4 favor tokens wins.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Win threshold** — First to 3 favor tokens wins.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Win threshold** — First to 3 favor tokens wins.

#### Tie and Multi-Winner Rules
- **Simultaneous winners** — Multiple players can win the game simultaneously.
- **Further tiebreaker** — No additional tiebreaker is specified beyond simultaneous victory allowance.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Round Priorities
- **Track discards immediately** — Start a visible mental count of all faceup cards from turn 1.
- **Guard with evidence** — Make Guard calls only after narrowing possibilities from discards and effects.
- **Use Priest for certainty** — Prioritize Priest information before taking high-variance elimination lines.
- **Sequence Handmaid** — Play Handmaid right before likely targeting windows, not automatically on first chance.
- **Protect hand value timing** — As deck thins, preserve high-value cards for possible reveal tiebreaks.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Mistakes
- **Random Guard naming** — Guessing blindly wastes Love Letter's highest-frequency elimination tool.
- **Countess miss** — Forgetting forced Countess play when paired with King/Prince breaks core rules.
- **Information leakage** — Over-talking after Priest reveals gives opponents your deduction path.
- **Bad Handmaid timing** — Using Handmaid when nobody can target you wastes protection value.
- **Spy neglect** — Ignoring Spy bonus costs free tokens in close races.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Information vs Tempo
- **Info line** — Use Priest/Baron first to reduce uncertainty before committing Guard/Prince pressure.
- **Tempo line** — Push immediate elimination attempts when table state already narrows likely hands.

#### Decision 2: Defense vs Pressure
- **Defense line** — Hold/play Handmaid to survive high-threat turns.
- **Pressure line** — Spend action on Prince/Guard to force outs even if it exposes your own plan.

#### Decision 3: End-of-Round Control
- **Showdown line** — Preserve high hand value when deck is near empty.
- **Knockout line** — Push elimination effects before reveal comparison can happen.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first readability with explicit Step 0 player-count audit and exact per-count headers pulled from the provided Love Letter rulebook text.
